name: Storybook MCP
what_it_does: MCP server providing AI agents with access to Storybook — the component development environment used by most frontend teams. Enables agents to browse component stories, read component documentation, inspect component props and variants, and reference design system components when generating or reviewing UI code.
use_cases: Browsing component library stories from UI development agents
Reading component API documentation and prop definitions from code generation agents
Referencing design system components when generating React/Vue/Angular code from coding agents
Validating component usage patterns from code review agents
not_for: Teams without Storybook in their development workflow
Generating Storybook stories (this reads existing stories, not creates them)
best_when: A frontend developer wants AI coding agents to reference their actual Storybook component library when generating or reviewing UI code — ensuring agents use established design system components correctly.
avoid_when: Your project doesn't use Storybook or doesn't have a component library — the MCP adds no value without an existing Storybook instance.

auth.notes: No authentication — connects to locally running Storybook instance. Storybook must be running (dev server or built static).

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP from mcpland. Storybook itself is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Storybook must be running locally — server unavailable when Storybook dev server is down
Storybook versions vary (6, 7, 8) — check compatibility with your Storybook version
Story discovery depends on consistent naming conventions in your Storybook setup
Proprietary/internal component docs visible to agents — ensure appropriate access control
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local access to Storybook stories and component documentation. Component library may contain proprietary design system information. Use only in development environments.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local access. Component library may be proprietary. Use in development environments only.
